x-enumeration-literals.name: Product Eligibility Test
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Checks whether a customer or account qualifies for a specific product or service based on defined criteria.

Comment:
Example: Verifying if a customer is eligible for a premium credit card based on income and credit score

x-enumeration-literals.name: Next Best Product Test
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Evaluates which alternative or complementary product is most suitable for a customer.

Comment:
Example: Suggesting a savings account with higher interest when the current account is underutilized.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Issued Device Test
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Verifies that a physical or digital device issued to a customer (card, token, mobile app) is correctly provisioned and functional.

Comment:
Example: Ensuring a newly issued debit card can be activated and used.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Card Holder Authentication Test
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Checks whether the cardholders identity is correctly verified during transactions.

Comment:
Example: PIN verification, OTP validation, biometric authentication.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Card Funds Available Test
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Confirms that sufficient funds or credit are available for a transaction.

Comment:
Example: Checking account balance or credit limit before approving a purchase.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Card Transaction Fraud Test
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Evaluates transactions to detect potentially fraudulent activity.

Comment:
Example: Flagging unusual international transactions or high-value purchases.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Card Stand-In Authorization Test
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Tests the stand-in authorization mechanism, allowing transactions to proceed when the central system is unavailable.

Comment:
Example: Offline transaction approvals during system downtime.

x-enumeration-literals.name: External Access Request Test
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Verifies whether external users or systems can access the system securely and with appropriate permissions.

Comment:
Example: Third-party API access to banking data for approved partners.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Suitability Test
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Assesses whether a product, service, or investment is appropriate for a customers needs, profile, or risk tolerance.

Comment:
Example: Recommending low-risk investment products to conservative investors.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Legal Compliance Test
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Checks adherence to laws and regulations relevant to the product, service, or system.

Comment:
Example: Ensuring anti-money laundering (AML) requirements are met.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Regulatory Compliance Test
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Evaluates compliance with industry or regulator-mandated rules.

Comment:
Example: Basel III capital adequacy requirements for banks.

x-enumeration-literals.name: System Functional Test
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Verifies that the system performs all specified functions correctly.

Comment:
Example: Ensuring account creation, fund transfer, and statement generation work as intended.

x-enumeration-literals.name: System Production Test
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Tests the system in a live or near-live environment to ensure readiness for actual operations.

Comment:
Example: Pilot run of transaction processing in production before full rollout.

x-enumeration-literals.name: System Business Risk Test
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Assesses potential risks to business operations arising from system behavior.

Comment:
Example: Testing the impact of system downtime on customer transactions.

x-enumeration-literals.name: System Operations Test
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Checks operational aspects such as backup, recovery, monitoring, and alerts.

Comment:
Example: Verifying failover procedures and system monitoring dashboards.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Credit Policy Compliance Test
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Checks whether credit decisions align with the banks credit policies and limits.

Comment:
Example: Verifying that loan approvals follow debt-to-income thresholds.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Rule Set and Decision Tree Test
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Tests the logic of automated rules and decision trees in decision-making systems.

Comment:
Example: Ensuring automated loan approval rules correctly evaluate risk factors.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Model Based Test
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Evaluates performance or predictions of mathematical or statistical models.

Comment:
Example: Stress testing a credit risk scoring model with historical data.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Asset and Liability Valuation Test
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Checks the accuracy of asset and liability valuations in financial statements.

Comment:
Example: Verifying fair value calculations for loans and investments.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Liquidity and Cash Flow Test
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Assesses whether sufficient liquidity and cash flow exist to meet obligations.

Comment:
Example: Testing banks daily liquidity coverage ratio under stress scenarios.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Risk Test
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Evaluates specific financial or operational risks.

Comment:
Example: Market risk, credit risk, or operational risk assessment.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Sensitivity Test
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Analyzes how changes in inputs affect outcomes, often for risk or financial models.

Comment:
Example: Measuring how interest rate changes affect loan portfolio value.
